TextENGNew: ``Well then, I'll be heading out.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_hudou2006. Thank you for the meal.``
``Not at all, not at all.@k@vS20/17/VTK2_oryou2002. Come visit again anytime.``
``I'm home!@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2002 Oh, Uncle Hiroshi!@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2003. It's been a while!``
``Hey there, Mion-chan.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_hudou2008. Heading home?@k@vS20/00/VTK2_hudou2009. I've got to drive the tour group back.``
``Drive them back?@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2005. Ah, for your real estate business?``
The Sonozaki family is one of the largest landholders in Hinamizawa.
Of course, since the land is all out here in the countryside, it simply sits idle rather than being put to any productive use.
Recently, some of the Sonozaki family's village properties had been put up for sale as residential lots.
At the family council, there were objections to selling off ancestral land to outsiders.@k But Granny barked 'Oh, shut it!' and that settled the matter.
changeReason: Old: 'Granny snapped and shut them right up'; New: 'Granny barked "Oh, shut it!" and that settled the matter'. New includes the actual quoted exclamation しゃあらしいわぁ (roughly 'how ridiculous!') which the old omits.

TextENGNew: Several fallow fields were cleared and opened up as lots available for purchase.
...I had my own questions about this, actually.
After all, the Sonozaki family is wealthy.
We have land to spare, and money to spare as well.
There's no reason we'd need to parcel off land for sale.
More than that—the idea of selling ancestral land to outsiders was the last thing I'd expect Granny to propose.
If anything, I'd have expected her to blow a blood vessel shouting down anyone who even suggested it.
``Well then, if you're ever near the train station, do stop by.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_hudou2011. I'll treat you to some barley tea at least.``
``Sure, thank you!@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2007. So how's business going?``
``I was surprised by how much interest the city folks have shown!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_hudou2013. They're mostly well-off types who live comfortably in the city but fancy having a country retreat.``
changeReason: Old: 'nouveau riche types'; New: 'well-off types'. Japanese 小金持ち means 'moderately wealthy' — not nouveau riche, which implies flashy new wealth.

TextENGNew: ``Well well, it takes all sorts!``
``Actually, one family isn't looking for a vacation home—they're considering a full move.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_hudou2015. Said something about wanting a studio, so they must be an artist of some kind.``
changeReason: Old: 'one family seeking to move here, not purchase a villa'; New: 'one family isn't looking for a vacation home—they're considering a full move'. Old says 'seeking to move' (definite); new says 'considering a full move' (tentative). Japanese 検討されてる means 'considering/deliberating'.

TextENGNew: ``Oh!@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2010. That's wonderful!``
``Oops, I'd better get going!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_hudou2017. Well, I need to get back to the bus!``
Apparently he had organized a viewing tour for the lots and was driving the bus for it.
It must be about time for the tour and explanations to wrap up.
Uncle Hiroshi ducked under a pine branch and trotted off.
changeReason: Old: 'He left in a hurry'; New: 'Uncle Hiroshi ducked under a pine branch and trotted off'. Japanese describes specific physical actions (松の枝をくぐって ducking under pine branches, ぱたぱたと trotting) that the old translation omits entirely.

TextENGNew: ``...Hey, can I ask you something?@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2012. You've always hated outsiders, Granny. So why did you decide to sell off land as building lots?``
``I despise outsiders and I despise city folk.@k@vS20/17/VTK2_oryou2004. ...But the village needs them.@k@vS20/17/VTK2_oryou2005. Think of it like airing out a room.``
``You're saying that bringing in people from outside will... have a positive effect on the village?``

``This is a fine village.@k@vS20/17/VTK2_oryou2007. ...The world moves fast these days, can't stay the same for even a single day.@k@vS20/17/VTK2_oryou2008. But in the old days, you could live here ten or twenty years and nothing would change. It was a quiet, unchanging village.``

``Well, that's just how country life is.@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2015. In this village, there's no difference between yesterday, today, and tomorrow.@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2016. ...Though I find it awfully boring!``

``But you made a friend, didn't you?@k@vS20/17/VTK2_oryou2010. Rena Ryuugu-chan transferred here from outside and the two of you hit it off.@k@vS20/17/VTK2_oryou2011. You told me how happy you were to finally have a friend close to your age.``
``Yeah.@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2018. Making an unexpected friend out of nowhere really is wonderful.``
``Before Rena-chan came, you kept whining that school was boring, boring, boring.@k@vS20/17/VTK2_oryou2013. Remember that?@k@vS20/17/VTK2_oryou2014. Keh keh keh keh!``
changeReason: Old: 'I remember you saying that school is boring when Rena-chan is absent'; New: 'Before Rena-chan came, you kept whining that school was boring, boring, boring'. Japanese レナちゃんが来んとな means 'before Rena came' (temporal), not 'when Rena is absent' (conditional).

TextENGNew: ``So you're saying that's the benefit of having people move here from outside?``

``...No matter how bitter the cold in winter, you still need to ventilate now and then, or the foul air from the hearth builds up and suffocates you.@k@vS20/17/VTK2_oryou2016. Sometimes you throw the windows wide open, knowing full well the freezing air will rush in.``
``...Welcoming people from outside is like ventilating the village?``
``Aye, it is.@k@vS20/17/VTK2_oryou2018. Let the clean air in, and the foul air—the likes of us—ought to be swept right out.``
If I so much as mention the Houjou family, Granny would fly into a rage, so I won't bring it up.
......But I suspect what Granny is really getting at encompasses everything—the undying animosity toward the Houjou family, all of it.
changeReason: Old: 'I think what Granny is trying to say includes things like their ongoing isolation'; New: 'I suspect what Granny is really getting at encompasses everything—the undying animosity toward the Houjou family, all of it'. New specifies the Houjou family animosity explicitly matching the Japanese 北条家に対する嫌悪の気風.

TextENGNew: Even now, remembering the explosive confrontation at the dam project meeting makes her blood boil.@k ...But I think even she feels it's time to sheathe the sword.
changeReason: Old: 'she gets really upset'; New: 'makes her blood boil'. Both convey anger but the more significant change is old: 'even she feels that needs to come to an end' vs new: 'even she feels it's time to sheathe the sword'. Japanese 鞘に収める means literally to sheathe a sword — the new metaphor is closer but both convey the same point. Not significant enough alone, but combined with the setup: old says 'what happened at that explanatory meeting' while new says 'the explosive confrontation at the dam project meeting' — the new adds specificity about which meeting (dam project).

TextENGNew: Of course, at her stubborn age, she can't easily bring herself to say she forgives them.
She can't publicly announce that it's time to let bygones be bygones.
......Honestly, she reminds me of an elementary school girl who actually likes someone but can't help being rude to their face.
...Perhaps when people get old enough, they come full circle and become children again.
And it's not just Granny.
The same goes for all the village elders.
...No, perhaps 'ingrained corruption' would be more apt.
changeReason: Old: 'the evil around here'; New: 'ingrained corruption'. Japanese 悪弊 means 'bad customs/corrupt practices', not general 'evil' — a more specific institutional concept.

TextENGNew: That is surely just what Granny meant by her metaphor of stale, foul air.
No amount of fanning will help in a sealed room.
Unless you open the windows and let in plenty of fresh air, the room will never clear.
``We don't know if anyone will actually move here, but@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2022. don't you think it's a lot to expect of whoever does?@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2023. Heh heh heh!``
``Just one girl—Rena-chan—transferring here changed you, Mion.@k@vS20/17/VTK2_oryou2020. No matter who moves here, the village will change.``
changeReason: Old ends without closing quote and drops the key detail; New: 'Just one girl—Rena-chan—transferring here changed you, Mion'. Old says 'Just Rena-chan moving here changed you' without the emphasis on 'one person' (１人) that the Japanese has.

TextENGNew: ``...Ideally, it'd be someone young and spirited—someone who'd cause such a ruckus that we old folk could rest easy handing the village over to them.``
changeReason: Old: 'I hope some young and energetic people who have the charisma to lead will appear'; New: 'Ideally, it'd be someone young and spirited—someone who'd cause such a ruckus that we old folk could rest easy handing the village over to them'. Japanese 大暴れ means 'causing a ruckus/wild activity' not 'charisma to lead'.

TextENGNew: ``The kind who'd make us old-timers say, 'Well, we've had enough—time to retire to the next world!'``
changeReason: Old: 'They need to be the ones to put us old people away into Heaven'; New: 'The kind who'd make us old-timers say, "Well, we've had enough—time to retire to the next world!"'. Japanese has the elders saying it themselves (言うくらいに) as direct speech, not outsiders doing it to them.

TextENGNew: ``Heh heh heh... what's that about?@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2025. Are you thinking about Grandpa?``
``You fool, who said anything about that old man?!@k@vS20/17/VTK2_oryou2024. Now go tell the housekeeper to clear the tea set from the veranda.``
``Yeah yeah.@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2027. Roger that.``
``...The day Satoshi Houjou disappeared.@k@vS20/17/VTK2_oryou2027. ...You confronted me about it, didn't you?``
``...Ah, yeah, I suppose I did.@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2030. ...Aha ha.``

``That...@k@vS20/17/VTK2_oryou2029. ...is what woke me up.@k@vS20/17/VTK2_oryou2030. ...This whole mess with the Houjou family—we can't just wait for us old folk to die and let time take care of it.@k@vS20/17/VTK2_oryou2031. ...It needs to be resolved soon, before it's too late.``
``But since you can't do it yourself,@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2032. ...you're entrusting it to an outsider?``
``......That is the best I can manage.``
``No matter how bad your hand is, a single good draw can turn the entire game around.@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2032b. ...Maybe it's worth drawing from the deck, just on that hope.``

``Your generation will be the new wind, Mion. Welcome the wind from outside.@k@vS20/17/VTK2_oryou2036. And you become the wind from within. Together, mingling as one, blow away the stagnation that plagues this village.``
``That's the height of leaving your problems to others.@k@vS20/03/VTK2_mion2034. You started this fire, and now you want your granddaughter to clean up the mess?``
``...Don't say that.@k@vS20/17/VTK2_oryou2038. As the new head of the Sonozaki family, welcome that fresh wind into the village.``
``...If that's the duty of the next family head, then...``
``...Please.@k@vS20/17/VTK2_oryou2040. Mion.@k@vS20/17/VTK2_oryou2041. I've grown old.@k@vS20/17/VTK2_oryou2042. All I can be now is the villain everyone despises.``
A wind chime gave a faint, fragile ring.
Surely it was a herald, announcing that a cool breeze was on its way...
